title: Power over Ethernet (PoE)
description: Delivers electrical power alongside data over standard Ethernet cables — eliminates separate power supplies.
fullExplanation: PoE allows network switches and injectors to deliver DC power to devices over the same Cat5e/Cat6 cable used for data. The Power Sourcing Equipment (PSE — usually a switch) detects a Powered Device (PD — IP camera, VoIP phone, WAP) using a signature resistance, negotiates power level, and delivers power. This eliminates the need for separate power outlets and adapters for network devices.

howItWorks.description: PSE applies low voltage to detect PD signature resistance (25kΩ)
PSE enables power delivery (48V DC nominal)
PD converts 48V to required voltage (5V, 12V, etc.)
PSE monitors current — disconnects if PD removed or fault detected

keyFacts: PoE uses 48V DC — safe for humans, efficient for transmission
Power delivered on data pairs (Alternative A) or spare pairs (Alternative B)
802.3bt uses all 4 pairs for power delivery
PoE budget: total switch PoE capacity (e.g. 370W for 24-port switch)
Midspan injector: adds PoE to non-PoE switch
PoE splitter: extracts power from PoE for non-PoE device
Cable quality matters: Cat5e minimum, Cat6 recommended for high-power PoE
